jokes.text: It's great to see so many happy smiles, most of them on mouth mutant.
jokes.setup: Typical wedding speech setup about happy guests
jokes.punchline: Subverted by the mutant context - literal mouth mutants have multiple smiles
jokes.explanation: Solid world-building humor that plays on the show's mutant characters. Good economy and character-specific context.

jokes.text: Even that year my side mutated into some kind of venomous radish.
jokes.setup: Sweet marriage speech about being by someone's side
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific mutation detail
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist specificity. 'Venomous radish' is wonderfully random and memorable. Perfect economy.

jokes.text: Well, not that much, actually, since mutants weren't allowed to leave the sewers until last year.
jokes.setup: Standard 'been through so much' anniversary sentiment
jokes.punchline: Undercuts the drama with practical limitation
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that deflates typical anniversary speech rhetoric with worldbuilding reality.

jokes.text: Like anyone wants to leave the sewer. Am I right?
jokes.setup: Leela mentions mutants couldn't leave sewers
jokes.punchline: Defensive rationalization from crowd
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment showing defensive mutant pride, good integration with the social dynamics.

jokes.text: I still have no idea.
jokes.setup: Video mentions Morris didn't know meaning of exolinguistics
jokes.punchline: He still doesn't know after 40 years
jokes.explanation: Perfect character consistency and economy. Shows Morris's lovable obliviousness spanning decades.

jokes.text: Oh, sugar lump, I hear you when you actually say something important.
jokes.setup: Munda frustrated he won't listen
jokes.punchline: Dismissive response that makes it worse
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment that reveals relationship tension through casual dismissiveness.

jokes.text: the lucrative pot-roast-making field. / Pot-roast-burning.
jokes.setup: Munda sarcastically mentions lucrative pot-roast career
jokes.punchline: Morris's blunt correction about her cooking skills
jokes.explanation: Excellent quick exchange that reveals both characters. Morris's oblivious honesty undercuts Munda's sarcasm perfectly.

jokes.text: I was going to surf the world's sewers, but I happily gave it up for you. Happily!
jokes.setup: Morris trying to show his sacrifice
jokes.punchline: The repeated 'Happily!' reveals his obvious resentment
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment. The emphatic repetition perfectly reveals his suppressed resentment despite claiming happiness.

jokes.text: Divorce is final.
jokes.setup: Leela trying to mediate the quarrel
jokes.punchline: Abrupt announcement delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection and economy. Perfect deadpan delivery that completely deflates Leela's mediation attempt.

jokes.text: You know, the rents are a lot more reasonable in Peru.
jokes.setup: Just announced divorce
jokes.punchline: Casual real estate observation
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully random non-sequitur that shows Morris's practical mindset even in emotional crisis.

jokes.text: I don't really have any use for these, but you can't have them back.
jokes.setup: Won cards in poker game
jokes.punchline: Spiteful logic of keeping useless winnings
jokes.explanation: Decent character beat showing petty gambling psychology, though not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: meeting a man made entirely of detergent.
jokes.setup: Munda marveling at space bar diversity
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of impossible alien physiology
jokes.explanation: Good world-building humor showing the casual absurdity of Futurama's universe through Munda's wonder.

jokes.text: Hey, I have an idea. Let's look in a different direction at something else.
jokes.setup: Munda notices Zapp Brannigan
jokes.punchline: Leela's desperate attempt to avoid him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leela moment - her awkward directness when trying to avoid Zapp. Good character integration.

jokes.text: That's a napkin dispenser, sir.
jokes.setup: Zapp addressing what he thinks is Admiral Chu
jokes.punchline: Kif's correction revealing Zapp's incompetence
jokes.explanation: Classic Zapp/Kif dynamic. Perfect character work showing Zapp's incompetence and Kif's weary corrections.

jokes.text: Last time you tried that, the Mexican restaurant declared war on us.
jokes.setup: Zapp wants to speak alien language without translator
jokes.punchline: Reference to previous diplomatic disaster
jokes.explanation: Excellent world-building joke that suggests Zapp's incompetence extends to Earth diplomacy. Very quotable.

jokes.text: I'd like to spank your sister with a slice of bologna.
jokes.setup: Zapp attempting to congratulate aliens in their language
jokes.punchline: Absurdly inappropriate mistranslation
jokes.explanation: Hilariously specific and inappropriate. Perfect Zapp moment - the absurd specificity of 'bologna' makes it memorable and quotable.

jokes.text: It's the battle of Paco's tacos all over again.
jokes.setup: Aliens drawing weapons after mistranslation
jokes.punchline: Reference to previous food-related diplomatic disaster
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that reinforces the pattern of Zapp's food-related diplomatic failures. Good world-building consistency.

jokes.text: just that Captain Brannigan confused the word for 'congratulations' with the word for 'spank your sister with bologna.'
jokes.setup: Leela asks what Munda said to calm the aliens
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact explanation of the absurd mistranslation
jokes.explanation: Good callback that gets a second laugh from the absurd phrase while showing Munda's competence.

jokes.text: So, uh, yesterday, huh? That business happened, right? Somebody?
jokes.setup: Zapp trying to justify business expense for drinks
jokes.punchline: Desperately vague attempt to create business context
jokes.explanation: Good Zapp characterization - shows his incompetent attempts at seeming professional and in control.

jokes.text: I don't know where most of her is, but her tongue's in Zapp's mouth.
jokes.setup: Leela asking where her mom went
jokes.punchline: Gross specificity about their make-out session
jokes.explanation: Good gross-out humor with efficient structure. The 'most of her' setup makes the punchline more effective.

jokes.text: Show me where he touched you on this dried-apple doll.
jokes.setup: Leela concerned about Zapp taking advantage
jokes.punchline: Absurd therapy/police interview parody with inappropriate prop
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist parody. The dried-apple doll is a perfect random substitution for the expected anatomical doll.

jokes.text: Did you know he wears a velour man-skirt?
jokes.setup: Munda praising Zapp's handsomeness
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific clothing detail presented as attractive
jokes.explanation: Good character detail that shows Munda's inexperience with dating and what she finds impressive about Zapp.

jokes.text: He showed me the commemorative painting.
jokes.setup: Leela reveals she slept with Zapp
jokes.punchline: Zapp has artwork documenting his conquests
jokes.explanation: Excellent Zapp characterization. The 'commemorative painting' is perfectly absurd and egotistical for his character.

jokes.text: Although he did say I'm a better kisser.
jokes.setup: Munda claiming this isn't a competition with Leela
jokes.punchline: Immediately makes it competitive with comparison
jokes.explanation: Good contradiction joke that shows how Munda is being manipulated by Zapp and creating awkwardness with Leela.

jokes.text: You know, without your mother, the sewer's a dank and dismal place.
jokes.setup: Morris expressing how much he misses Munda
jokes.punchline: Describes sewer as if it was pleasant before she left
jokes.explanation: Nice irony showing Morris's emotional state through the implication that the sewer was somehow not dank and dismal before.

jokes.text: I ought to punch him right in his big mouth. And then in all his smaller mouths!
jokes.setup: Morris angry about the other man
jokes.punchline: Escalates to absurd mutant-specific violence
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that works on multiple levels - shows Morris's anger while playing into the mutant physiology of his rival.

jokes.text: You can teach them how to surf, and they can make sure you don't... (cork pop, gulping, blubbering, gunshot)
jokes.setup: Suggesting Fry and Bender accompany Morris
jokes.punchline: Sound effects implying Morris might kill himself
jokes.explanation: Dark humor done well. The sound effects create the joke while avoiding explicit statement, showing concern for Morris's mental state.

jokes.text: I have to keep an eye on mom to make sure she and Zapp don't... (air raid siren, train chugging)
jokes.setup: Leela explaining why she can't go
jokes.punchline: Absurd sound effects to avoid saying 'have sex'
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the sound effects gag, showing Leela's discomfort with thinking about her mom's sex life.

jokes.text: You're too old to be happy.
jokes.setup: Munda asking why Leela can't let her be happy
jokes.punchline: Brutally direct ageist response
jokes.explanation: Shockingly direct and brutal. Perfect economy and character moment showing Leela's desperation to break them up.

jokes.text: Sex with Zapp! Ew! Sex with Zapp! Aah! Sex with Zapp! No, no!
jokes.setup: Munda mentions hot sex with Zapp
jokes.punchline: Leela's breakdown repeating the horrible concept
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Leela's psychological breakdown. The repetition effectively conveys her horror.

jokes.text: This one is for the E. Coli... And this one's for the leprechaunorrhea.
jokes.setup: Fry apparently getting treated for diseases
jokes.punchline: Made-up disease combining leprechaun and gonorrhea
jokes.explanation: Creative portmanteau that's both disgusting and imaginative. Good Futurama-style absurd medical condition.

jokes.text: I don't want to put a rat in your face-cage, or whatever you kids say nowadays
jokes.setup: Morris trying to use modern slang
jokes.punchline: Completely made-up phrase that makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurd phrase that perfectly captures an out-of-touch parent trying to use youth slang. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Right on, brah! I feel free as a bum.
jokes.setup: Morris has embraced sewer surfing culture
jokes.punchline: His attempt at surfer slang results in 'free as a bum' instead of 'free as a bird'
jokes.explanation: Good character transformation showing Morris's new laid-back attitude with appropriate malapropism

jokes.text: Leela, does your mother know you're using her pole?
jokes.setup: Leela attempting pole-based seduction
jokes.punchline: Zapp's oblivious literal interpretation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Zapp's obliviousness to Leela's seduction attempt, treating it as property concern.

jokes.text: She is one juicy calamari.
jokes.setup: Zapp describing his love for Munda
jokes.punchline: Food metaphor for describing a tentacled woman
jokes.explanation: Good Zapp moment that's both gross and character-appropriate. The food metaphor fits his crude personality.

jokes.text: Will I marry you?
jokes.setup: Standard proposal setup
jokes.punchline: Backwards proposal asking if he'll marry her
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zapp moment showing his narcissism even in romantic gestures. Good misdirection and economy.

jokes.text: Just call me... 'Daddy.'
jokes.setup: Leela calling Zapp names
jokes.punchline: Zapp's inappropriate step-father power play
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe Zapp moment that perfectly captures his ability to make any situation more uncomfortable. Good escalation.

jokes.text: Probably, it'll be a filthy nurse.
jokes.setup: Threatening to put Munda in nursing home
jokes.punchline: Casual cruel detail about what will hit her there
jokes.explanation: Dark humor showing Leela's desperation escalating to cruel threats. The casual delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: He seems like a nice kahuna.
jokes.setup: Morris commenting on Zapp
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate surfer slang for describing a space captain
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency showing Morris's new surfer personality affecting his vocabulary even in serious situations.

jokes.text: There'll be 'chicken or fish, circle one.'
jokes.setup: Trying to convince Leela to attend wedding
jokes.punchline: Unappetizing description of typical wedding food choice
jokes.explanation: Mild observational humor about standard wedding reception food. Functional but not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: I was riding the el torito dinner backwash, and I thought I'd pop in.
jokes.setup: Morris explains how he happened to visit the bar
jokes.punchline: The absurd specificity of surfing restaurant backwash with a Spanish name
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully specific and absurd world-building that shows Morris's new lifestyle

jokes.text: So I will now join myself in holy macaroni.
jokes.setup: Zapp conducting his own wedding ceremony
jokes.punchline: Food-based mispronunciation of 'matrimony'
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay showing Zapp's incompetence at formal procedures. The food reference fits his character.

jokes.text: Zapp, you magnificent bastard, do you take Munda to be your lawful...
jokes.setup: Wedding ceremony format
jokes.punchline: Zapp complimenting himself during his own ceremony
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zapp narcissism moment. Can't even conduct a ceremony without praising himself. Good character integration.

jokes.text: Oh, Mr. Agnew? (Monster-like grunting)
jokes.setup: Nixon trying to do comedy routine with Agnew
jokes.punchline: Agnew just grunts like a monster
jokes.explanation: Good sight gag playing on Futurama's weird version of historical figures. Shows Agnew as literally monstrous.

jokes.text: I come from the sewer, but that is low.
jokes.setup: Zapp's deceptive war plan revealed
jokes.punchline: Sewer dweller calling something else low
jokes.explanation: Excellent irony with perfect character integration. Munda using her literal low position to judge moral low behavior. Great economy and structure.

jokes.text: Zapp... lying sack of...
jokes.setup: Munda speaks to the aliens in their language to tell the truth
jokes.punchline: The translation shows her calling Zapp a 'lying sack of' something
jokes.explanation: Functional but not exceptional - shows Munda's anger through translation device

jokes.text: and you can't return ice cream cakes!
jokes.setup: Zapp ordering return fire and the cake
jokes.punchline: Kif's practical objection to returning dessert
jokes.explanation: Good Kif moment showing his literal interpretation of Zapp's commands even in crisis situations.

jokes.text: But then I conquered my inner peace.
jokes.setup: Morris explaining his change from zen attitude
jokes.punchline: Contradiction of zen philosophy presented as achievement
jokes.explanation: Excellent philosophical contradiction showing Morris choosing action over acceptance. Perfect character arc moment.

jokes.text: Hold on to your coconuts. We're going for the ride of a lifetime!
jokes.setup: Morris about to surf the spaceship hull
jokes.punchline: Surfer slang in deadly space situation
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency showing Morris applying surfer mentality to space heroics. 'Coconuts' is absurd but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: If heaven's above us, then the whole universe is just God's glorious sewer.
jokes.setup: Morris explaining how he overcame fear of space
jokes.punchline: Reframing the universe through sewer-dweller perspective
jokes.explanation: Brilliant philosophical reframing that's both touching and funny. Perfect character integration showing Morris finding cosmic perspective through his sewer origins. Highly quotable.
